issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on image
issuesFound.explanation: The element inside the logo link has an alt attribute, but the link already provides an accessible name via aria-label. However, the image is decorative and should not convey information. Since the image is the sole content of the link and the link has an aria-label, the image is redundant for screen readers. But per rules, if alt is present (even alt=""), we do not suggest adding aria-label. However, the image has alt="Luminary Studio" which is not empty and duplicates the link's accessible name. This is not a violation per se, but the image is not decorative in the sense that it has a non-empty alt. However, the link already has an accessible name, so the image's alt is not needed for accessibility and could be considered redundant. But per rule 8: if an already has an alt attribute (even alt=""), do NOT suggest adding aria-label — alt fully provides the accessible name. Here, alt is present and not empty, so no issue is reported for missing alt. However, the image is inside a link that already has an accessible name, so the image's alt is not necessary and may cause verbosity. But WCAG does not require removing redundant alt text. Therefore, no issue is reported for this image.
